1	# Unified Non-Level-2 Refresh With Legacy CLI Deprecation
     2	
     3	## Summary
     4	Refactor `quant-build-production-dataset` into the single canonical refresh entrypoint for all non-Level-2 data, then deprecate the old direct refresh/build CLIs after the unified path no longer shells out to them. Default behavior remains **daily-first**: resolve the latest completed SSE trading day, refresh daily/reference data, build and gate execution-ready artifacts, and build/gate ML-ready training artifacts only when the label exit horizon is complete.
     5	
     6	## Key Changes
     7	- Make `quant-build-production-dataset` the only default public refresh command:
     8	  - optional `--end-date`; omitted resolves to the latest completed SSE open day using Asia/Shanghai time, the SSE trading calendar, and an after-close cutoff
     9	  - generated labels and all derived paths come from a single `RefreshContext`, with no hardcoded `20260424` orchestration paths or downstream builder defaults
    10	  - `--dry-run` emits the full step plan, resolved dates, labels, explicit input/output paths, input hashes when files already exist, skip reasons, and checkpoint reuse decisions
    11	  - `--strict-full` fails if ML-ready training labels cannot be completed
    12	  - `--mode daily-refresh|monthly-refresh|full-refresh` makes operator intent explicit while keeping one public entrypoint
    13	  - `--skip-baostock` reuses existing audited BaoStock/patch artifacts only; it fails if required active-identity artifacts are absent and records freshness/status
    14	  - `--enable-gdelt-doc-api` opts into slower GDELT document diagnostics; default daily and monthly refreshes keep the current DOC API disabled behavior and record skipped status plus any reused macro/event inputs
    15	- Orchestrate all non-Level-2 families in dependency order:
    16	  - Tushare validation, endpoint freshness probes, and latest-date resolution
    17	  - stock master/BaoStock/sentinels
    18	  - daily A-share store, ETF universe/store/reference
    19	  - benchmark import/source audit, constituents, and returns
    20	  - fundamentals, PIT fundamentals, official industry manifest validation/reuse, conditional official industry manifest discovery/build, PIT industry, non-L2 reference
    21	  - regimes, macro/events with GDELT DOC diagnostics only when explicitly enabled, noise layer
    22	  - execution-ready build/gate
    23	  - ML-ready build/gate only when `label_complete_through` covers the monthly signal; otherwise the latest month is prediction-only or excluded from gated training labels and the ML step emits skipped output metadata instead of training artifacts
    24	- Add a shared internal orchestration layer so the unified command does not shell out to public CLIs. Old CLI modules keep current behavior until the production command is migrated, then call the shared step functions only after an explicit bypass flag.
    25	- Add checkpoint/resume semantics before broad deprecation lands:
    26	  - stable step IDs
    27	  - started/completed timestamps
    28	  - normalized inputs, input hashes, output paths, and output hashes
    29	  - `rebuilt`, `reused_existing`, `skipped`, `stale`, `failed`, and `gated` statuses
    30	  - resume decisions based on matching context labels, paths, hashes, and step versions
    31	
    32	## Implementation Sequence
    33	1. Add date/context resolution, dry-run schema, freshness registry, and checkpoint metadata while keeping existing public CLIs callable.
    34	2. Extract shared callable step APIs for each builder/refresher/gate so the public CLI wrappers and production orchestrator can share implementation without subprocess invocation. This step must land before any direct CLI wrapper requires `--allow-manual-data-step`.
    35	3. Migrate `quant-build-production-dataset` to the shared step APIs and verify no production execution path calls deprecated console script names, direct public CLI `main()` functions, or `uv run` command lists.
    36	4. Add `--allow-manual-data-step` guards to direct data-writing CLI wrappers only after the production command is self-contained.
    37	5. Update docs and deprecation inventory after guard behavior and bypass tests pass.
    38	
    39	## Refresh Context
    40	- Introduce one immutable `RefreshContext` created by `quant-build-production-dataset` before any step runs:
    41	  - `start_date`
    42	  - `requested_end_date`
    43	  - `daily_end_date`
    44	  - `feature_snapshot_end`
    45	  - `entry_data_end`
    46	  - `label_complete_through`
    47	  - `monthly_signal_end`
    48	  - `latest_rebalance_data_date`
    49	  - `ml_training_label_end`
    50	  - `prediction_only_signal_end`
    51	  - generated label set for daily, monthly, execution-ready, ML-ready, benchmark, ETF proxy, regime, macro/event, PIT fundamentals, PIT industry, and non-L2 reference artifacts, including skipped-output labels for artifacts that are not materialized in the selected mode
    52	  - all derived input/output paths for every builder and gate
    53	  - command mode, skip flags, bypass policy, and checkpoint location
    54	- Every orchestrated step must receive explicit context paths and labels. No step may rely on its standalone CLI defaults for production orchestration.
    55	- Manifest output must record the actual input paths, input hashes, output paths, output hashes, generated labels, source date coverage, source staleness, and whether each artifact was rebuilt or reused.
    56	- Production manifests and tests must verify recorded paths and hashes use the generated label for the requested refresh, not stale defaults from `20260424` or any other prior run.
    57	- `feature_snapshot_end` is the latest month-end whose monthly features can be computed.
    58	- `entry_data_end` is the latest rebalance entry date with complete open-price inputs.
    59	- `label_complete_through` is the latest month-end whose label exit horizon has complete exit-price data. If `monthly_signal_end` is later than `label_complete_through`, that month must be marked `prediction_only` or excluded from gated ML training output; it must not silently produce partial labels.
    60	- When ML-ready training is skipped, `RefreshContext` still emits the intended ML-ready label/path plus a `skipped` step status and reason. The orchestrator must not create or advertise a completed ML-ready training artifact for that label.
    61	
    62	## Date Resolution and Freshness
    63	- Date resolution uses Asia/Shanghai local date/time and the SSE official trading calendar.
    64	- If `--end-date` is omitted:
    65	  - before the configured market-data completion cutoff, use the latest completed SSE open day strictly before today
    66	  - after the cutoff, use today only if today is an SSE open day and required endpoint freshness probes confirm complete data
    67	  - on weekends and SSE holidays, snap backward to the prior completed SSE open day
    68	- If `--end-date` is provided:
    69	  - preserve it as `requested_end_date`
    70	  - set `daily_end_date` to that date only when it is an open day with complete endpoint freshness
    71	  - otherwise snap `daily_end_date` backward to the prior completed open day and record `requested_end_date_status=closed_or_incomplete`
    72	  - fail only when `--strict-full` or an explicit future date requires data that cannot be available
    73	- The resolver must include a fixture where `today=2026-05-02` resolves `daily_end_date=20260430`, because SSE lists Labor Day closure from May 1 through May 5, 2026 and reopening on May 6.
    74	- Extend Tushare validation beyond stock-basic access with an endpoint freshness registry. For each endpoint family, declare requiredness by mode (`daily-refresh`, `monthly-refresh`, `full-refresh`), expected data lag, date field used for freshness, retry/backoff policy, stale status text, and whether staleness blocks daily execution, monthly execution, full refresh only, or only marks an optional artifact stale.
    75	- Daily live execution must block on stale daily A-share store inputs, daily_basic, moneyflow, limit data, ETF daily, and any benchmark data required by execution-ready outputs. Slower or lower-priority non-Level-2 families must block only the modes/artifacts that consume them, so optional or monthly-only freshness failures cannot make routine daily refresh flaky.
    76	- Fund adjustment, fund NAV, benchmark import/source files, benchmark constituents/returns, fundamentals, PIT fundamentals, official industry manifests/source files, macro/event, regime, noise, and other configured non-Level-2 families must be explicitly classified in the registry instead of covered by one broad all-or-nothing freshness gate.
    77	- Freshness decisions must cover raw endpoint probes and derived local inputs. Derived inputs such as pinned official industry manifests, imported benchmark constituent sources, and reused macro/event artifacts must record path, hash, coverage, source staleness, and consuming artifacts before live steps run.
    78	
    79	## Legacy CLI Deprecation
    80	- Deprecate direct data-refresh/build entrypoints by requiring `--allow-manual-data-step` when run directly:
    81	  - `quant-build-monthly-history`, `quant-build-universe-history`, `quant-build-etf-universe`
    82	  - `quant-backfill-store`, `quant-backfill-etf-store`, `quant-backfill-etf-reference`
    83	  - `quant-backfill-fundamentals`, `quant-backfill-non-l2-reference`
    84	  - `quant-backfill-benchmark-constituents`, `quant-backfill-benchmark-returns`
    85	  - `quant-import-benchmark-constituents`
    86	  - `quant-build-etf-proxy-returns`, `quant-build-regime-tags`, `quant-build-macro-event-layer`
    87	  - `quant-build-noise-layer`, `quant-build-ml-dataset`, `quant-build-fundamental-features`
    88	  - `quant-build-pit-industry`, `quant-build-official-industry-manifest`
    89	  - `quant-build-baostock-reference`, `quant-build-baostock-sentinels`
    90	  - `quant-build-execution-ready-dataset`
    91	- If `quant-import-benchmark-constituents` is kept as an official manual import instead of a refresh command, document that distinction and still require `--allow-manual-data-step` plus explicit source/path arguments.
    92	- Keep validation/gate commands active and not deprecated:
    93	  - `quant-validate-tushare`
    94	  - `quant-validate-store`
    95	  - `quant-gate-ml-dataset`
    96	  - `quant-gate-execution-ready-dataset`
    97	- Direct deprecated CLIs should fail fast only after the implementation sequence has migrated production orchestration to shared step APIs. The failure message must include:
    98	  - replacement command: `uv run quant-build-production-dataset`
    99	  - escape hatch: rerun with `--allow-manual-data-step`
   100	  - clear note that manual runs are for troubleshooting, diagnostics, or isolated reruns only
   101	- `quant-build-execution-ready-dataset` is a builder, so it is internal/orchestrator-only by default and requires `--allow-manual-data-step` when invoked directly. `quant-gate-execution-ready-dataset` remains unrestricted.
   102	- Manual bypass runs must preserve current standalone behavior only when real arguments are provided explicitly. Add a central helper that each guarded CLI calls to declare required explicit arguments under `--allow-manual-data-step`; production-sensitive defaults for ML dataset inputs, execution-ready inputs, fundamental feature dates, benchmark imports, and all output paths must not be accepted silently in bypass mode.
   103	- The shared bypass helper must produce a per-CLI contract used by implementation and tests: argument names that must be explicit, defaults that are forbidden in bypass mode, artifact paths that must be recorded in JSON, and whether the CLI is a refresh/build/import category.
   104	- `quant-build-official-industry-manifest` is part of the unified orchestration because PIT industry consumes its source manifest, but default daily and monthly refreshes should reuse a pinned manifest after validating coverage, source staleness, hashes, and path existence. Discovery/download/build is allowed by default only when the pinned manifest is missing, coverage is insufficient for the requested context, staleness exceeds the registry threshold, or `--mode full-refresh` explicitly requests a refreshable external-source pass. If an operator bypass reuses an existing manifest, output JSON must record manifest path, source period coverage, source staleness, hash, and reuse status.
   105	- GDELT DOC API default behavior is opt-in. Without `--enable-gdelt-doc-api`, missing or stale DOC diagnostics are recorded as `skipped_optional` and must not fail daily/monthly macro/event builds; reused macro/event inputs still record path, hash, coverage, and staleness. With `--enable-gdelt-doc-api`, DOC API failures follow the macro/event freshness registry classification for the selected mode.
   106	- Update `AGENTS.md`, `docs/60_deprecation_inventory.md`, and current preprocessing docs so the unified command is the only documented default route.
   107	
   108	## Public Interface
   109	```bash
   110	uv run quant-build-production-dataset
   111	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --end-date 20260430
   112	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --dry-run
   113	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --strict-full
   114	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --mode daily-refresh
   115	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --mode monthly-refresh
   116	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --mode full-refresh
   117	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --skip-baostock
   118	uv run quant-build-production-dataset --enable-gdelt-doc-api
   119	```
   120	
   121	Output JSON includes `requested_end_date`, `daily_end_date`, `requested_end_date_status`, `feature_snapshot_end`, `entry_data_end`, `label_complete_through`, `monthly_signal_end`, `monthly_boundary_status`, `prediction_only_signal_end`, command mode, generated labels/paths, endpoint and derived-input freshness decisions by family, per-step statuses, checkpoint decisions, skipped/reused/stale/failed/gated states, execution-ready gate status, and ML-ready status.
   122	
   123	For `--skip-baostock`, output JSON must still include the active stock-universe identity source, patched master path, audited patch source path, sentinel status, freshness, and whether the required artifacts were reused.
   124	
   125	## Test Plan
   126	- Dry-run tests:
   127	  - generated plan contains no stale `20260424` paths for a new end date
   128	  - all non-Level-2 data families appear by default
   129	  - generated plan includes official industry manifest validation/reuse before PIT industry, with discovery/build only when pinned coverage or staleness checks require it or full-refresh requests it
   130	  - generated plan includes explicit generated inputs for production constants, ML dataset, execution-ready, ETF proxy, macro regimes, benchmark, labels, and gates
   131	  - ML-ready training output is skipped with a clear reason when `label_complete_through` does not cover `monthly_signal_end`
   132	  - latest month is marked prediction-only or excluded from gated training labels when entry data exists but label exit data is incomplete
   133	  - default macro/events dry-run records GDELT DOC API diagnostics as skipped unless `--enable-gdelt-doc-api` is present
   134	  - dry-run fixture covers a month-end-complete date
   135	  - dry-run fixture covers an incomplete monthly boundary
   136	- Deprecation tests:
   137	  - deprecation guards are added only after production orchestration uses shared step APIs instead of public CLI subprocesses
   138	  - each deprecated direct CLI exits nonzero without `--allow-manual-data-step`
   139	  - deprecated direct CLIs run their existing behavior with the bypass flag only when the central helper confirms all required real arguments were provided explicitly
   140	  - `quant-build-execution-ready-dataset` requires `--allow-manual-data-step` when run directly
   141	  - bypass runs require explicit real arguments for production-sensitive paths and do not fall back to stale defaults
   142	  - central bypass helper enforces each CLI's required explicit arguments, including ML dataset inputs, execution-ready inputs, fundamental feature dates, benchmark imports, and output paths
   143	  - `quant-import-benchmark-constituents` is guarded as a data-writing manual step, or documented as an official import that still requires bypass plus explicit required arguments
   144	  - gate/validation commands remain callable without bypass
   145	- Resolver and behavior tests:
   146	  - holiday/weekend resolves to prior completed open day
   147	  - `today=2026-05-02` resolves `daily_end_date=20260430`
   148	  - explicit closed `--end-date` preserves `requested_end_date`, snaps `daily_end_date` backward, and records the closed/incomplete status
   149	  - endpoint freshness registry blocks only the modes/artifacts that require the stale family, with daily blockers covering daily, daily_basic, moneyflow, limits, ETF daily, and execution-ready benchmark requirements
   150	  - slower or optional families such as fund_adj, fund_nav, official industry discovery, macro/event DOC diagnostics, and monthly-only non-Level-2 families do not block daily-refresh unless their consuming artifact is requested
   151	  - derived-input freshness records path, hash, coverage, and staleness for pinned official industry manifests, benchmark import sources, and reused macro/event inputs
   152	  - `--strict-full` fails when ML-ready would be skipped
   153	  - daily-first mode can succeed with execution-ready gated and ML skipped
   154	- Integration tests:
   155	  - production orchestration uses an internal API with stubbed steps instead of `uv run` subprocess command lists
   156	  - no deprecated console script names appear in the production execution path
   157	  - every step receives explicit paths from `RefreshContext`
   158	  - ML labels are complete only through `label_complete_through`; incomplete latest-month labels are excluded from gated training artifacts
   159	  - default daily/monthly macro/event orchestration does not call the GDELT DOC API without `--enable-gdelt-doc-api`
   160	  - output schema distinguishes `skipped`, `reused_existing`, `stale`, `failed`, and `gated`
   161	  - partial daily-first success records execution-ready success and ML-ready skip without marking the full run failed
   162	  - manifest tests assert recorded input paths and hashes match the generated refresh label
   163	  - checkpoint/resume tests reuse only when step IDs, labels, paths, hashes, and step versions match
   164	- Quality gates:
   165	  ```bash
   166	  uv run ruff check src tests
   167	  uv run ruff format --check src tests
   168	  uv run pyright
   169	  uv run bandit -c pyproject.toml -r src/quantresearch
   170	  uv run --group test pytest -q
   171	  ```
   172	
   173	## Assumptions
   174	- “Deprecate” means direct manual CLIs remain available only behind `--allow-manual-data-step`, not removed from `pyproject.toml`.
   175	- `quant-build-production-dataset` remains the public command name.
   176	- Level-2 remains excluded.
   177	- Canonical research-run pointers are not updated automatically.
   178	- Unresolved questions: none.

429	def build_execution_manifest(
   430	    input_paths: dict[str, Path],
   431	    output_paths: dict[str, Path],
   432	    *,
   433	    output_label: str,
   434	    stock_rows: int,
   435	    etf_rows: int,
   436	    calendar_rows: int,
   437	    stock_date_range: dict[str, str],
   438	    etf_date_range: dict[str, str],
   439	    etf_reference_coverage: dict[str, float],
   440	) -> dict[str, Any]:
   441	    def file_info(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
   442	        return {
   443	            "path": str(path),
   444	            "exists": path.exists(),
   445	            "size_bytes": path.stat().st_size if path.exists() else 0,
   446	            "sha256": file_sha256(path) if path.exists() and path.is_file() else "",
   447	        }
   448	
   449	    return {
   450	        "output_label": output_label,
   451	        "contract_versions": {"execution_ready": "v1"},
   452	        "files": {name: file_info(path) for name, path in input_paths.items()},
   453	        "outputs": {name: file_info(path) for name, path in output_paths.items()},
   454	        "row_counts": {
   455	            "daily_stock_execution": stock_rows,
   456	            "daily_etf_execution": etf_rows,
   457	            "rebalance_calendar": calendar_rows,
   458	        },
   459	        "date_ranges": {"stock_daily": stock_date_range, "etf_daily": etf_date_range},
   460	        "policy_versions": {
   461	            "source_boundary": "curated_daily_panels_plus_gated_monthly_ml_ready",
   462	            "daily_weekly_research_gate": "requires_quant_gate_execution_ready_dataset",
   463	            "stock_price_basis": "raw_ohlc_plus_same_day_adjusted_ohlc",
   464	            "stock_moneyflow_policy": "propagate_curated_daily_order_size_moneyflow_when_available",
   465	            "stock_pit_industry_policy": "latest_known_capco_csrc_l1_by_trade_date_when_available",
   466	            "etf_price_basis": "adjusted_when_tushare_fund_adj_available_else_raw_diagnostic_only",
   467	            "etf_raw_price_only_policy": "diagnostic_only_not_promotable",
   468	            "premium_discount_policy": "eod_nav_diagnostic_when_available",
   469	        },
   470	        "coverage": {"etf_reference": etf_reference_coverage},
   471	    }
   472	
   473	
   474	def _read_manifest(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
   475	    if not path.exists():
   476	        return {}
   477	    return json.loads(path.read_text())
   478	
   479	
   480	def _issue(issues: list[GateIssue], severity: str, code: str, message: str) -> None:
   481	    issues.append(GateIssue(severity=severity, code=code, message=message))
   482	
   483	
   484	def _parquet_count(path: Path) -> int:
   485	    if not path.exists():
   486	        return 0
   487	    return int(pl.scan_parquet(path).select(pl.len().alias("n")).collect().item())
   488	
   489	
   490	def _duplicate_key_count(path: Path, keys: list[str]) -> int:
   491	    if not path.exists():
   492	        return 0
   493	    return int(
   494	        pl.scan_parquet(path)
   495	        .group_by(keys)
   496	        .len()
   497	        .filter(pl.col("len") > 1)
   498	        .select((pl.col("len") - 1).sum().fill_null(0).alias("n"))
   499	        .collect()
   500	        .item()
   501	    )
   502	
   503	
   504	def validate_execution_ready_dataset(execution_ready_dir: Path) -> ExecutionReadyGateResult:
   505	    issues: list[GateIssue] = []
   506	    execution_ready_dir = execution_ready_dir.expanduser()
   507	    for filename in EXECUTION_READY_ARTIFACTS:
   508	        path = execution_ready_dir / filename
   509	        if not path.exists():
   510	            _issue(issues, "hard_fail", "missing_artifact", f"missing required artifact: {path}")
   511	
   512	    stock_path = execution_ready_dir / "daily_stock_execution.parquet"
   513	    etf_path = execution_ready_dir / "daily_etf_execution.parquet"
   514	    calendar_path = execution_ready_dir / "rebalance_calendar.parquet"
   515	    manifest_path = execution_ready_dir / "execution_manifest.json"
   516	    stock_rows = _parquet_count(stock_path)
   517	    etf_rows = _parquet_count(etf_path)
   518	    calendar_rows = _parquet_count(calendar_path)
   519	    duplicate_stock_keys = _duplicate_key_count(stock_path, ["ts_code", "trade_date"])
   520	    duplicate_etf_keys = _duplicate_key_count(etf_path, ["ts_code", "trade_date"])
   521	
   522	    if stock_rows <= 0:
   523	        _issue(issues, "hard_fail", "empty_stock_execution", "daily_stock_execution.parquet is empty")
   524	    if etf_rows <= 0:
   525	        _issue(issues, "hard_fail", "empty_etf_execution", "daily_etf_execution.parquet is empty")
   526	    if calendar_rows <= 0:
   527	        _issue(issues, "hard_fail", "empty_rebalance_calendar", "rebalance_calendar.parquet is empty")
   528	    if duplicate_stock_keys:
   529	        _issue(issues, "hard_fail", "duplicate_stock_execution_keys", f"duplicate stock execution keys: {duplicate_stock_keys}")
   530	    if duplicate_etf_keys:
   531	        _issue(issues, "hard_fail", "duplicate_etf_execution_keys", f"duplicate ETF execution keys: {duplicate_etf_keys}")
   532	
   533	    if stock_path.exists():
   534	        stock_schema = set(pl.scan_parquet(stock_path).collect_schema().names())
   535	        required_stock = {
   536	            "ts_code",
   537	            "trade_date",
   538	            "adj_open",
   539	            "adj_close",
   540	            "investable_daily_member",
   541	            "entry_1d_available",
   542	            "execution_reason_codes",
   543	        }
   544	        missing = sorted(required_stock - stock_schema)
   545	        if missing:
   546	            _issue(issues, "hard_fail", "stock_missing_required_columns", f"stock execution missing columns: {missing}")
   547	        else:
   548	            missing_moneyflow = sorted(set(STOCK_MONEYFLOW_COLUMNS) - stock_schema)
   549	            if missing_moneyflow:
   550	                _issue(
   551	                    issues,
   552	                    "warn",
   553	                    "stock_moneyflow_columns_missing",
   554	                    f"stock execution missing optional moneyflow columns: {missing_moneyflow}",
   555	                )
   556	            missing_industry = sorted({"industry_l1_code", "industry_l1_name", "industry_source_reliability"} - stock_schema)
   557	            if missing_industry:
   558	                _issue(
   559	                    issues,
   560	                    "warn",
   561	                    "stock_pit_industry_columns_missing",
   562	                    f"stock execution missing optional PIT industry columns: {missing_industry}",
   563	                )
   564	            bad_entry = (
   565	                pl.scan_parquet(stock_path)
   566	                .filter(
   567	                    pl.col("entry_1d_available")
   568	                    & (pl.col("fwd_1d_is_suspended").fill_null(False) | (pl.col("fwd_1d_open") >= pl.col("fwd_1d_up_limit") * 0.999999))
   569	                )
   570	                .select(pl.len().alias("n"))
   571	                .collect()
   572	                .item()
   573	            )
   574	            if int(bad_entry):
   575	                _issue(
   576	                    issues, "hard_fail", "invalid_stock_entry_available", f"entry_1d_available violates suspension/limit gate: {bad_entry}"
   577	                )
   578	
   579	    if etf_path.exists():
   580	        etf_schema = set(pl.scan_parquet(etf_path).collect_schema().names())
   581	        required_etf = {
   582	            "ts_code",
   583	            "trade_date",
   584	            "adj_open",
   585	            "adj_close",
   586	            "adj_factor_available",
   587	            "etf_reference_status",
   588	            "etf_whitelist_eligible",
   589	            "etf_execution_reason_codes",
   590	        }
   591	        missing = sorted(required_etf - etf_schema)
   592	        if missing:
   593	            _issue(issues, "hard_fail", "etf_missing_required_columns", f"ETF execution missing columns: {missing}")
   594	        else:
   595	            invalid_eligible = (
   596	                pl.scan_parquet(etf_path)
   597	                .filter(pl.col("etf_whitelist_eligible") & pl.col("etf_reference_status").eq("raw_price_only_unadjusted"))
   598	                .select(pl.len().alias("n"))
   599	                .collect()
   600	                .item()
   601	            )
   602	            if int(invalid_eligible):
   603	                _issue(issues, "hard_fail", "raw_price_etf_marked_eligible", f"raw-price-only ETFs marked eligible: {invalid_eligible}")
   604	
   605	    manifest = _read_manifest(manifest_path)
   606	    if manifest.get("contract_versions", {}).get("execution_ready") != "v1":
   607	        _issue(issues, "hard_fail", "manifest_contract_mismatch", "execution_manifest.json must declare execution_ready v1")
   608	    coverage = manifest.get("coverage", {}).get("etf_reference", {}) if manifest else {}
   609	    adj_code_coverage = float(coverage.get("adj_factor_code_coverage", 0.0) or 0.0)
   610	    nav_code_coverage = float(coverage.get("premium_discount_code_coverage", 0.0) or 0.0)
   611	    if adj_code_coverage < 0.95:
   612	        _issue(issues, "warn", "low_etf_adj_factor_coverage", f"ETF adj_factor code coverage is {adj_code_coverage:.2%}")
   613	    if nav_code_coverage < 0.95:
   614	        _issue(issues, "warn", "low_etf_nav_coverage", f"ETF premium/NAV code coverage is {nav_code_coverage:.2%}")
   615	
   616	    hard_fails = sum(1 for issue in issues if issue.severity == "hard_fail")
   617	    return ExecutionReadyGateResult(
   618	        status="passed" if hard_fails == 0 else "failed",
   619	        issues=tuple(issues),
   620	        stock_rows=stock_rows,
   621	        etf_rows=etf_rows,
   622	        calendar_rows=calendar_rows,
   623	        duplicate_stock_keys=duplicate_stock_keys,
   624	        duplicate_etf_keys=duplicate_etf_keys,
   625	    )

430	def backfill_reduced_universe_store(
   431	    pro,
   432	    layout: DataLakeLayout,
   433	    history_with_universes: pd.DataFrame,
   434	    start_date: str,
   435	    end_date: str,
   436	    scope_name: str = "a_share_all_v1",
   437	    pause_seconds: float = 0.25,
   438	    universe_columns: tuple[str, ...] = (
   439	        "universe_liq_top800",
   440	        "universe_csi300_proxy_mainboard",
   441	        "universe_csi500_proxy_mainboard",
   442	    ),
   443	    symbol_scope: str = "universe_columns",
   444	    stock_master: pd.DataFrame | None = None,
   445	) -> BackfillResult:
   446	    if stock_master is not None:
   447	        master = stock_master.copy()
   448	    else:
   449	        master = fetch_csi_a_share_master(pro) if symbol_scope == "all_a_share" else fetch_mainboard_master(pro)
   450	    master_codes = set(master["ts_code"].astype(str))
   451	    if symbol_scope == "universe_columns":
   452	        scoped_history = _history_for_backfill_window(history_with_universes, start_date=start_date, end_date=end_date)
   453	        if scoped_history.empty:
   454	            raise ValueError(f"history_with_universes has no signal_date rows in requested backfill window {start_date}-{end_date}")
   455	        selected = build_selected_symbols_for_columns(scoped_history, universe_columns=universe_columns)
   456	        notes = f"Reduced-universe daily-path backfill for {', '.join(universe_columns)}."
   457	    elif symbol_scope == "all_mainboard":
   458	        selected = build_all_mainboard_symbols(master, start_date=start_date, end_date=end_date)
   459	        notes = "Full mainboard daily-path backfill for all listed or delisted names active during the requested window."
   460	    elif symbol_scope == "all_a_share":
   461	        selected = build_all_stock_symbols(master, start_date=start_date, end_date=end_date)
   462	        notes = "Full SSE/SZSE A-share daily-path backfill for mainboard, ChiNext, and STAR names active during the requested window."
   463	    else:
   464	        raise ValueError(f"unsupported symbol_scope: {symbol_scope}")
   465	    selected_codes = set(selected["ts_code"].astype(str))
   466	    scoped_codes = selected_codes & master_codes
   467	
   468	    conn = connect_manifest(layout)
   469	    run_id = start_run(
   470	        conn=conn,
   471	        scope_name=scope_name,
   472	        start_date=start_date,
   473	        end_date=end_date,
   474	        notes=notes,
   475	    )
   476	
   477	    dataset_records: list[dict[str, str | int]] = []
   478	    try:
   479	        master_frame = master[master["ts_code"].astype(str).isin(scoped_codes)].copy()
   480	        master_frame["as_of_date"] = end_date
   481	        master_path = _write_dataset(
   482	            conn=conn,
   483	            run_id=run_id,
   484	            layout=layout,
   485	            scope_name=scope_name,
   486	            dataset_name="mainboard_master",
   487	            start_date=end_date,
   488	            end_date=end_date,
   489	            frame=master_frame,
   490	        )
   491	        dataset_records.append({"dataset_name": "mainboard_master", "path": str(master_path), "rows": int(len(master_frame))})
   492	
   493	        selected_path = _write_dataset(
   494	            conn=conn,
   495	            run_id=run_id,
   496	            layout=layout,
   497	            scope_name=scope_name,
   498	            dataset_name="selected_symbols",
   499	            start_date=end_date,
   500	            end_date=end_date,
   501	            frame=selected,
   502	        )
   503	        dataset_records.append({"dataset_name": "selected_symbols", "path": str(selected_path), "rows": int(len(selected))})
   504	
   505	        trade_cal = _call_with_retry(
   506	            pro.trade_cal,
   507	            exchange="SSE",
   508	            start_date=start_date,
   509	            end_date=end_date,
   510	            fields="exchange,cal_date,is_open,pretrade_date",
   511	        ).copy()
   512	        trade_path = _write_dataset(
   513	            conn=conn,
   514	            run_id=run_id,
   515	            layout=layout,
   516	            scope_name=scope_name,
   517	            dataset_name="trade_cal",
   518	            start_date=start_date,
   519	            end_date=end_date,
   520	            frame=trade_cal,
   521	        )
   522	        dataset_records.append({"dataset_name": "trade_cal", "path": str(trade_path), "rows": int(len(trade_cal))})
   523	
   524	        namechange = fetch_namechange_history(pro, start_date=start_date, end_date=end_date)
   525	        namechange = namechange[namechange["ts_code"].astype(str).isin(scoped_codes)].copy()
   526	        namechange_path = _write_dataset(
   527	            conn=conn,
   528	            run_id=run_id,
   529	            layout=layout,
   530	            scope_name=scope_name,
   531	            dataset_name="namechange",
   532	            start_date=start_date,
   533	            end_date=end_date,
   534	            frame=namechange,
   535	        )
   536	        dataset_records.append({"dataset_name": "namechange", "path": str(namechange_path), "rows": int(len(namechange))})
   537	
   538	        open_trade_dates = trade_cal.loc[trade_cal["is_open"] == 1, "cal_date"].astype(str).sort_values().tolist()
   539	        dense_datasets = ("daily", "daily_basic", "moneyflow", "stk_limit", "adj_factor")
   540	        for trade_date in open_trade_dates:
   541	            daily_codes: set[str] = set()
   542	            for dataset_name in dense_datasets:
   543	                frame = _fetch_endpoint_trade_date(pro, dataset_name=dataset_name, trade_date=trade_date)
   544	                frame = _filter_codes(frame, scoped_codes)
   545	                if dataset_name == "daily":
   546	                    daily_codes = set(frame["ts_code"].astype(str)) if "ts_code" in frame.columns else set()
   547	                if dataset_name == "moneyflow" and frame.empty and daily_codes:
   548	                    frame = _empty_moneyflow_frame(daily_codes, trade_date=trade_date)
   549	                path = _write_dataset(
   550	                    conn=conn,
   551	                    run_id=run_id,
   552	                    layout=layout,
   553	                    scope_name=scope_name,
   554	                    dataset_name=dataset_name,
   555	                    start_date=trade_date,
   556	                    end_date=trade_date,
   557	                    frame=frame,
   558	                )
   559	                dataset_records.append({"dataset_name": dataset_name, "path": str(path), "rows": int(len(frame))})
   560	                time.sleep(pause_seconds)
   561	        for chunk_start, chunk_end in month_chunks(start_date, end_date):
   562	            frame = _fetch_endpoint(pro, dataset_name="suspend_d", start_date=chunk_start, end_date=chunk_end)
   563	            frame = _filter_codes(frame, scoped_codes)
   564	            path = _write_dataset(
   565	                conn=conn,
   566	                run_id=run_id,
   567	                layout=layout,
   568	                scope_name=scope_name,
   569	                dataset_name="suspend_d",
   570	                start_date=chunk_start,
   571	                end_date=chunk_end,
   572	                frame=frame,
   573	            )
   574	            dataset_records.append({"dataset_name": "suspend_d", "path": str(path), "rows": int(len(frame))})
   575	            time.sleep(pause_seconds)
   576	        finish_run(conn, run_id=run_id, status="completed")
   577	    except Exception as exc:
   578	        finish_run(conn, run_id=run_id, status="failed", notes=str(exc))
   579	        raise
   580	    finally:
   581	        conn.close()
   582	
   583	    return BackfillResult(
   584	        run_id=run_id,
   585	        scope_name=scope_name,

   588	    )
   589	
   590	
   591	def backfill_fund_store(
   592	    pro,
   593	    layout: DataLakeLayout,
   594	    fund_codes: tuple[str, ...],
   595	    start_date: str,
   596	    end_date: str,
   597	    scope_name: str = "etf_core_v1",
   598	    pause_seconds: float = 0.1,
   599	) -> BackfillResult:
   600	    conn = connect_manifest(layout)
   601	    run_id = start_run(
   602	        conn=conn,
   603	        scope_name=scope_name,
   604	        start_date=start_date,
   605	        end_date=end_date,
   606	        notes="ETF core daily backfill for benchmark and hybrid comparison.",
   607	    )
   608	    dataset_records: list[dict[str, str | int]] = []
   609	    try:
   610	        trade_cal = _call_with_retry(
   611	            pro.trade_cal,
   612	            exchange="SSE",
   613	            start_date=start_date,
   614	            end_date=end_date,
   615	            fields="exchange,cal_date,is_open,pretrade_date",
   616	        ).copy()
   617	        trade_path = _write_dataset(
   618	            conn=conn,
   619	            run_id=run_id,
   620	            layout=layout,
   621	            scope_name=scope_name,
   622	            dataset_name="trade_cal",
   623	            start_date=start_date,
   624	            end_date=end_date,
   625	            frame=trade_cal,
   626	        )
   627	        dataset_records.append({"dataset_name": "trade_cal", "path": str(trade_path), "rows": int(len(trade_cal))})
   628	
   629	        for code in fund_codes:
   630	            for chunk_start, chunk_end in year_chunks(start_date, end_date):
   631	                frame = _call_with_retry(
   632	                    pro.fund_daily,
   633	                    ts_code=code,
   634	                    start_date=chunk_start,
   635	                    end_date=chunk_end,
   636	                    fields="ts_code,trade_date,open,close,amount",
   637	                ).copy()
   638	                path = _write_dataset(
   639	                    conn=conn,
   640	                    run_id=run_id,
   641	                    layout=layout,
   642	                    scope_name=scope_name,
   643	                    dataset_name="fund_daily",
   644	                    start_date=chunk_start,
   645	                    end_date=chunk_end,
   646	                    frame=frame,
   647	                    suffix=code,
   648	                )
   649	                dataset_records.append({"dataset_name": "fund_daily", "path": str(path), "rows": int(len(frame))})
   650	                time.sleep(pause_seconds)
   651	        finish_run(conn, run_id=run_id, status="completed")
   652	    except Exception as exc:
   653	        finish_run(conn, run_id=run_id, status="failed", notes=str(exc))
   654	        raise
   655	    finally:
   656	        conn.close()
   657	
   658	    return BackfillResult(
   659	        run_id=run_id,
   660	        scope_name=scope_name,
   661	        datasets=pd.DataFrame(dataset_records),
   662	        symbol_count=int(len(fund_codes)),
   663	    )

    18	    parser.add_argument("start_date", help="Backfill start date in YYYYMMDD format")
    19	    parser.add_argument("end_date", help="Backfill end date in YYYYMMDD format")
    20	    parser.add_argument(
    21	        "--history-with-universes",
    22	        default="",

    24	    )

    28	        "--universe-columns",
    29	        default="universe_liq_top800,universe_csi300_proxy_mainboard,universe_csi500_proxy_mainboard",
    30	        help="Comma-separated universe columns to union into the symbol scope.",
    31	    )
    32	    parser.add_argument(

    34	        choices=("universe_columns", "all_mainboard", "all_a_share"),
    35	        default="all_a_share",
    36	        help="Use the universe-column union, all Main Board names, or all SSE/SZSE A-share names active in the backfill window.",
    37	    )
    38	    parser.add_argument(

    42	    )
    43	    args = parser.parse_args()

    51	    )
    52	    history = pd.read_csv(history_csv)
    53	    pro = create_pro_client(settings.tushare_token)
    54	    stock_master_path = resolve_project_path(settings.project_root, args.stock_master_override) if args.stock_master_override else None
    55	    stock_master = load_stock_master_override(stock_master_path) if stock_master_path is not None else None
    56	    result = backfill_reduced_universe_store(
    57	        pro=pro,
    58	        layout=layout,
    59	        history_with_universes=history,
    60	        start_date=args.start_date,
    61	        end_date=args.end_date,
    62	        scope_name=args.scope_name,
    63	        pause_seconds=args.pause_seconds,
    64	        universe_columns=tuple(col.strip() for col in args.universe_columns.split(",") if col.strip()),
    65	        symbol_scope=args.symbol_scope,
    66	        stock_master=stock_master,
    67	    )
    68	
    69	    payload = {

    71	        "scope_name": result.scope_name,
    72	        "symbol_count": result.symbol_count,
    73	        "stock_master_override": str(stock_master_path) if stock_master_path is not None else "",
    74	        "stock_master_rows": int(len(stock_master)) if stock_master is not None else None,
    75	        "datasets": grouped_sum_records(result.datasets, group_by="dataset_name", value="rows"),
    76	        "data_lake_root": str(layout.root),
    77	        "manifest_db": str(layout.manifest_db),
    78	    }
    79	    print(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
    80	    return 0

1	from __future__ import annotations
     2	
     3	import argparse
     4	import json
     5	
     6	from quantresearch.core.config import load_settings, resolve_project_path
     7	from quantresearch.data.store import create_layout
     8	from quantresearch.preprocessing.fundamentals import build_monthly_pit_fundamental_features, build_pit_normalized_fundamentals
     9	
    10	
    11	def main() -> int:
    12	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Build PIT-normalized fundamentals and monthly all-A-share fundamental features.")
    13	    parser.add_argument("--fund-scope", default="a_share_all_v1_fundamentals")
    14	    parser.add_argument("--panel-path", default="data_lake/curated/a_share_all_v1/daily_panel_a_share_all_v1.parquet")
    15	    parser.add_argument("--monthly-history", default="output/data/a_share_monthly_history_universes_20080101_20260424.csv")
    16	    parser.add_argument("--end-date", default="20260424")
    17	    parser.add_argument("--pit-output-label", default="a_share_all_v1")
    18	    parser.add_argument("--feature-output-label", default="a_share_all_v1")
    19	    args = parser.parse_args()
    20	
    21	    settings = load_settings(require_token=False)
    22	    layout = create_layout(settings)
    23	    pit_output_dir = settings.curated_root / "fundamentals_pit_v1" / args.pit_output_label
    24	    feature_output_dir = settings.curated_root / "fundamental_features_v1" / args.feature_output_label
    25	    pit_paths, pit_qa = build_pit_normalized_fundamentals(layout, args.fund_scope, pit_output_dir)
    26	    features_path, feature_summary = build_monthly_pit_fundamental_features(
    27	        layout=layout,
    28	        scope_name=args.fund_scope,
    29	        panel_path=resolve_project_path(settings.project_root, args.panel_path),
    30	        monthly_history_path=resolve_project_path(settings.project_root, args.monthly_history),
    31	        output_dir=feature_output_dir,
    32	        end_date=args.end_date,
    33	    )
    34	    payload = {

    22	def _read_official_sources(path_text: str, project_root) -> tuple[pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame]:
    23	    if not path_text:
    24	        return pd.DataFrame(), pd.DataFrame()
    25	    manifest_path = resolve_project_path(project_root, path_text)
    26	    if not manifest_path.exists():
    27	        raise FileNotFoundError(f"official source manifest not found: {manifest_path}")
    28	    frames = []
    29	    audit_rows = []
    30	    for source in load_official_source_manifest(manifest_path):
    31	        source_path = source.path if source.path.is_absolute() else resolve_project_path(project_root, source.path)
    32	        if not source_path.exists():
    33	            audit_rows.append(
    34	                {
    35	                    "source_file": str(source_path),
    36	                    "period_label": source.period_label,
    37	                    "period_end_date": source.period_end_date,
    38	                    "publication_date": source.publication_date,
    39	                    "status": "missing_file",
    40	                    "rows": 0,
    41	                }
    42	            )
    43	            continue
    44	        if source_path.suffix.lower() not in {".pdf", ".doc", ".docx", ".xls", ".xlsx"}:
    45	            audit_rows.append(
    46	                {
    47	                    "source_file": str(source_path),
    48	                    "period_label": source.period_label,
    49	                    "period_end_date": source.period_end_date,
    50	                    "publication_date": source.publication_date,
    51	                    "status": "unsupported_source_type",
    52	                    "rows": 0,
    53	                }
    54	            )
    55	            continue
    56	        frame = parse_official_industry_source(
    57	            type(source)(
    58	                path=source_path,
    59	                period_label=source.period_label,
    60	                period_end_date=source.period_end_date,
    61	                publication_date=source.publication_date,
    62	                source_url=source.source_url,
    63	            )
    64	        )
    65	        frames.append(frame)
    66	        audit_rows.append(
    67	            {
    68	                "source_file": str(source_path),
    69	                "period_label": source.period_label,
    70	                "period_end_date": source.period_end_date,
    71	                "publication_date": source.publication_date,
    72	                "status": "parsed" if not frame.empty else "parsed_empty",
    73	                "rows": int(len(frame)),
    74	            }
    75	        )
    76	    official = pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True) if frames else pd.DataFrame()
    77	    return official, pd.DataFrame(audit_rows)

    80	def _fetch_baostock_history(start_date: str, end_date: str, frequency: str) -> tuple[pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame]:
    81	    periods = period_end_schedule(start_date, end_date, frequency=frequency)
    82	    frames = []
    83	    audit_rows = []
    84	    for row in periods.itertuples(index=False):
    85	        try:
    86	            print(f"BaoStock industry snapshot {row.period_label} {row.period_end_date}", file=sys.stderr, flush=True)
    87	            raw = fetch_baostock_industry_snapshot(str(row.period_end_date))
    88	            normalized = normalize_baostock_industry_snapshot(
    89	                raw,
    90	                period_label=str(row.period_label),
    91	                period_end_date=str(row.period_end_date),
    92	            )
    93	            frames.append(normalized)
    94	            audit_rows.append(
    95	                {
    96	                    "period_label": str(row.period_label),
    97	                    "period_end_date": str(row.period_end_date),
    98	                    "status": "parsed",
    99	                    "raw_rows": int(len(raw)),
   100	                    "usable_rows": int(len(normalized)),
   101	                    "known_at_min": str(normalized["known_at"].min()) if not normalized.empty else "",
   102	                    "known_at_max": str(normalized["known_at"].max()) if not normalized.empty else "",
   103	                }
   104	            )
   105	        except Exception as exc:
   106	            audit_rows.append(
   107	                {
   108	                    "period_label": str(row.period_label),
   109	                    "period_end_date": str(row.period_end_date),
   110	                    "status": f"error:{type(exc).__name__}",
   111	                    "raw_rows": 0,
   112	                    "usable_rows": 0,
   113	                    "known_at_min": "",
   114	                    "known_at_max": "",
   115	                    "error": str(exc),
   116	                }
   117	            )
   118	    history = pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True) if frames else pd.DataFrame()
   119	    return history, pd.DataFrame(audit_rows)

   122	def main() -> int:
   123	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Build PIT CAPCO/CSRC L1 industry classification history.")
   124	    parser.add_argument("--official-source-manifest", default="", help="CSV with official source files and publication metadata.")
   125	    parser.add_argument("--start-date", default="20121231")
   126	    parser.add_argument("--end-date", default="20260424")
   127	    parser.add_argument("--baostock-frequency", choices=("quarterly", "halfyear"), default="quarterly")
   128	    parser.add_argument("--no-baostock", action="store_true", help="Disable BaoStock gap-fill/cross-check snapshots.")
   129	    parser.add_argument("--no-baostock-gap-fill", action="store_true", help="Use BaoStock only for cross-check audits, not selected rows.")
   130	    parser.add_argument("--output-label", default="capco_csrc_l1")
   131	    args = parser.parse_args()
   132	
   133	    settings = load_settings(require_token=False)
   134	    official, official_audit = _read_official_sources(args.official_source_manifest, settings.project_root)
   135	    if args.no_baostock:
   136	        baostock = pd.DataFrame()
   137	        baostock_audit = pd.DataFrame()
   138	    else:
   139	        baostock, baostock_audit = _fetch_baostock_history(args.start_date, args.end_date, args.baostock_frequency)
   140	    history, source_audit = build_industry_history(
   141	        official=official,
   142	        baostock=baostock,
   143	        allow_baostock_gap_fill=not args.no_baostock_gap_fill,
   144	    )
   145	    crosscheck = build_industry_crosscheck_audit(official, baostock)
   146	    output_dir = settings.curated_root / "industry_classification_v1" / args.output_label
   147	    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   148	    history_path = output_dir / "industry_history.parquet"
   149	    source_audit_path = output_dir / "industry_source_audit.csv"
   150	    official_audit_path = output_dir / "official_source_parse_audit.csv"
   151	    baostock_audit_path = output_dir / "baostock_source_audit.csv"
   152	    crosscheck_path = output_dir / "industry_crosscheck_audit.csv"
   153	    policy_path = output_dir / "industry_policy.json"
   154	    history.to_parquet(history_path, index=False)
   155	    source_audit.to_csv(source_audit_path, index=False)
   156	    official_audit.to_csv(official_audit_path, index=False)
   157	    baostock_audit.to_csv(baostock_audit_path, index=False)
   158	    crosscheck.to_csv(crosscheck_path, index=False)
   159	    policy = {
   160	        "classification_system": "CAPCO_CSRC_L1",
   161	        "canonical_source": "official_capco_csrc",
   162	        "baostock_allowed": not args.no_baostock,
   163	        "baostock_gap_fill_allowed": not args.no_baostock_gap_fill,
   164	        "known_at_policy": "official_publication_date_or_baostock_updateDate",
   165	        "neutralization_default": "disabled_until_explicit_ablation",
   166	    }
   167	    policy_path.write_text(json.dumps(policy, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
   168	    payload = {
   169	        "status": "completed",
   170	        "rows": int(len(history)),
   171	        "periods": int(history["period_end_date"].nunique()) if not history.empty else 0,
   172	        "stocks": int(history["ts_code"].nunique()) if not history.empty else 0,
   173	        "official_rows": int(len(official)),
   174	        "baostock_rows": int(len(baostock)),
   175	        "paths": {
   176	            "industry_history": str(history_path),
   177	            "industry_source_audit": str(source_audit_path),
   178	            "official_source_parse_audit": str(official_audit_path),
   179	            "baostock_source_audit": str(baostock_audit_path),
   180	            "industry_crosscheck_audit": str(crosscheck_path),
   181	            "industry_policy": str(policy_path),
   182	        },
   183	    }
   184	    print(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2, default=str))
   185	    return 0
   186	
   187	
   188	if __name__ == "__main__":
   189	    raise SystemExit(main())

    46	class _AnchorParser(HTMLParser):
    47	    def __init__(self) -> None:
    48	        super().__init__()
    49	        self._in_anchor = False
    50	        self._href = ""
    51	        self._text_parts: list[str] = []
    52	        self.links: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
    53	
    54	    def handle_starttag(self, tag: str, attrs: list[tuple[str, str | None]]) -> None:
    55	        if tag.lower() != "a":
    56	            return
    57	        self._in_anchor = True
    58	        self._href = dict(attrs).get("href") or ""
    59	        self._text_parts = []
    60	
    61	    def handle_data(self, data: str) -> None:
    62	        if self._in_anchor:
    63	            self._text_parts.append(data)
    64	
    65	    def handle_endtag(self, tag: str) -> None:
    66	        if tag.lower() != "a" or not self._in_anchor:
    67	            return
    68	        text = " ".join("".join(self._text_parts).split())
    69	        self.links.append((text, self._href))
    70	        self._in_anchor = False
    71	        self._href = ""
    72	        self._text_parts = []
    73	
    74	
    75	def _fetch_bytes(url: str, *, timeout_seconds: int = 30) -> bytes:
    76	    request = Request(url, headers={"User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0"})
    77	    with urlopen(request, timeout=timeout_seconds) as response:
    78	        return response.read()
    79	
    80	
    81	def _fetch_html(url: str, *, timeout_seconds: int = 30) -> str:
    82	    return _fetch_bytes(url, timeout_seconds=timeout_seconds).decode("utf-8", errors="ignore")
    83	
    84	
    85	def _extract_links(html: str, base_url: str) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
    86	    parser = _AnchorParser()
    87	    parser.feed(html)
    88	    return [(text, urljoin(base_url, href.strip())) for text, href in parser.links if href.strip()]
    89

    91	def infer_period_from_title(title: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
    92	    compact = "".join(str(title or "").split())
    93	    half_match = re.search(r"(?P<year>20\d{2})年(?P<half>上|下)半年", compact)
    94	    if half_match:
    95	        year = half_match.group("year")
    96	        half = half_match.group("half")
    97	        return (f"{year}H1", f"{year}0630") if half == "上" else (f"{year}H2", f"{year}1231")
    98	    quarter_match = re.search(r"(?P<year>20\d{2})年第?(?P<quarter>[1-4])季度", compact)
    99	    if quarter_match:
   100	        year = quarter_match.group("year")
   101	        quarter = int(quarter_match.group("quarter"))
   102	        month_day = {1: "0331", 2: "0630", 3: "0930", 4: "1231"}[quarter]
   103	        return f"{year}Q{quarter}", f"{year}{month_day}"
   104	    return "", ""
   105	
   106	
   107	def extract_publication_date(html: str) -> str:
   108	    for pattern in (r"发布时间[:：]\s*(?P<date>\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})", r"日期[:：]\s*(?P<date>\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})"):
   109	        match = re.search(pattern, html)
   110	        if match:
   111	            return match.group("date").replace("-", "")
   112	    return ""
   113	
   114	
   115	def _is_pdf_url(url: str) -> bool:
   116	    return urlparse(url).path.lower().endswith(".pdf")
   117	
   118	
   119	def _is_supported_source_url(url: str) -> bool:
   120	    return Path(urlparse(url).path).suffix.lower() in {".pdf", ".doc", ".docx", ".xls", ".xlsx"}
   121	
   122

   124	    pdf_links = [(text, url) for text, url in links if _is_pdf_url(url)]

   137	    non_code_sorted = [(text, url) for text, url in pdf_links if "股票代码" not in text and "gupiaodaima" not in url.lower()]

   139

    45	    "holding_period_return",
    46	    "executable_return",
    47	    "one_month_signal_return",
    48	    "exclude_from_label",
    49	    "label_reason_codes",

    55	def build_sample_manifest(
    56	    input_paths: dict[str, Path],
    57	    *,
    58	    output_label: str,
    59	    contract_versions: dict[str, str] | None = None,
    60	    row_counts: dict[str, int] | None = None,
    61	    date_ranges: dict[str, dict[str, str]] | None = None,
    62	    policy_versions: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
    63	) -> dict[str, Any]:
    64	    files: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
    65	    for name, path in input_paths.items():
    66	        files[name] = {
    67	            "path": str(path),
    68	            "exists": path.exists(),
    69	            "size_bytes": path.stat().st_size if path.exists() else 0,
    70	            "sha256": file_sha256(path) if path.exists() and path.is_file() else "",
    71	        }
    72	    return {
    73	        "output_label": output_label,
    74	        "files": files,
    75	        "contract_versions": contract_versions or {},
    76	        "row_counts": row_counts or {},
    77	        "date_ranges": date_ranges or {},
    78	        "policy_versions": policy_versions or {},
    79	    }
    80	
    81	
    82	def _date_range(frame: pd.DataFrame, col: str) -> dict[str, str]:
    83	    if frame.empty or col not in frame.columns:
    84	        return {"start": "", "end": ""}
    85	    values = frame[col].dropna().astype(str)
    86	    return {"start": str(values.min()) if not values.empty else "", "end": str(values.max()) if not values.empty else ""}
    87	
    88	
    89	def build_feature_coverage(features: pd.DataFrame, *, key_cols: tuple[str, ...] = ("ts_code", "signal_date")) -> pd.DataFrame:
    90	    rows = []
    91	    for col in features.columns:
    92	        if col in key_cols:
    93	            continue
    94	        rows.append(
    95	            {
    96	                "column": col,
    97	                "non_null_rows": int(features[col].notna().sum()),
    98	                "null_rows": int(features[col].isna().sum()),
    99	                "coverage_rate": float(features[col].notna().mean()) if len(features) else 0.0,
   100	                "dtype": str(features[col].dtype),
   101	            }
   102	        )
   103	    return pd.DataFrame(rows)
   104	
   105	
   106	def leakage_prone_feature_columns(frame: pd.DataFrame) -> list[str]:
   107	    blocked: list[str] = []
   108	    for col in frame.columns:
   109	        col_text = str(col)
   110	        if col_text in LEAKAGE_PRONE_FEATURE_COLUMNS:
   111	            blocked.append(col_text)
   112	            continue
   113	        if col_text.startswith(LEAKAGE_PRONE_FEATURE_COLUMN_PREFIXES):
   114	            blocked.append(col_text)
   115	            continue
   116	        if col_text.startswith("benchmark_") and "_return" in col_text:
   117	            blocked.append(col_text)
   118	    return blocked
   119	
   120	
   121	def drop_leakage_prone_feature_columns(frame: pd.DataFrame) -> tuple[pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame]:
   122	    blocked = leakage_prone_feature_columns(frame)
   123	    audit = pd.DataFrame(
   124	        [{"column": col, "policy_action": "dropped_from_features", "reason": "future_execution_or_label_field"} for col in blocked]
   125	    )
   126	    if not blocked:
   127	        return frame.copy(), audit
   128	    return frame.drop(columns=blocked, errors="ignore").copy(), audit
   129	
   130

    23	def _cost_scenario(name: str) -> CostScenario:
    24	    scenarios = {scenario.name: scenario for scenario in default_cost_scenarios()}
    25	    if name not in scenarios:
    26	        raise ValueError(f"unknown cost scenario: {name}")
    27	    return scenarios[name]

    30	def _normalize_yyyymmdd_series(series: pd.Series) -> pd.Series:
    31	    text = series.fillna("").astype(str).str.strip().str.replace(r"\.0$", "", regex=True)
    32	    parsed = pd.to_datetime(text, format="%Y%m%d", errors="coerce")
    33	    return parsed.dt.strftime("%Y%m%d")
    34

    36	def _trade_rate_cost(entry_date: object, exit_date: object, scenario: CostScenario, slippage_bps: float) -> tuple[float, float, float]:
    37	    slippage = slippage_bps / 10000.0
    38	    buy_rate = scenario.buy_broker_rate + scenario.buy_other_rate + slippage
    39	    sell_rate = scenario.sell_broker_rate + effective_sell_other_rate(scenario, exit_date or entry_date) + slippage
    40	    return buy_rate, sell_rate, buy_rate + sell_rate

    86	    out["sell_cost_rate"] = [item[1] for item in rates]
    87	    out["round_trip_cost_rate"] = [item[2] for item in rates]
    88	    out["executable_return"] = (1.0 + out["holding_period_return"]) * (1.0 - out["sell_cost_rate"]) / (1.0 + out["buy_cost_rate"]) - 1.0

{"summary":"Formal read-only review completed. I inspected the plan, the target file, current CLI wiring, production and execution gates, ML label construction, non-L2 backfill code, deprecation helpers, docs, and verified the 2026 SSE Labor Day fixture against SSE sources.","markdown_response":"**Findings**

1. **High: Daily default may accidentally become a slow, flaky full non-L2 rebuild.**
The plan says the unified command should orchestrate all non-Level-2 families in dependency order and the dry-run should include all families by default ([data-plan.md](/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md:15), [data-plan.md](/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md:128)). In the current code, some of those families are heavy per-code or external-source jobs: non-L2 reference loops across all active stock codes and endpoint batches with sleeps ([non_l2_reference.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/data/non_l2_reference.py:318), [non_l2_reference.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/data/non_l2_reference.py:227)), and official industry discovery performs live CAPCO/CSRC downloads ([official_sources.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/preprocessing/official_sources.py:151)). This conflicts with the daily-first goal. Make the mode matrix explicit per family: `execute`, `probe`, `reuse_existing`, `skip_optional`, or `block_if_stale`. Default `daily-refresh` should execute only daily/execution-required inputs and reuse or mark slow monthly/full families.

2. **High: Execution-ready success is not cleanly decoupled from ML-ready skip.**
The plan allows daily-first success with execution-ready gated and ML-ready skipped ([data-plan.md](/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md:153)), but the existing execution-ready builder still records `ml_ready_dir` as an input ([cli_build_execution_ready_dataset.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_execution_ready_dataset.py:76), [cli_build_execution_ready_dataset.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_execution_ready_dataset.py:131)) and declares its source boundary as `curated_daily_panels_plus_gated_monthly_ml_ready` ([execution_ready.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/preprocessing/execution_ready.py:461)). If the current ML label is skipped, the execution manifest can imply a gated ML dependency that does not exist. Either remove ML-ready from execution-ready inputs for daily execution, or record a separate `training_context_ml_ready_dir` that points to the last known gated ML artifact while the current label is `skipped`.

3. **High: Label completeness needs a stricter artifact boundary, not only row flags.**
The plan says incomplete latest-month labels should be prediction-only or excluded ([data-plan.md](/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md:59)), but the current ML builder can still materialize rows with missing exit prices: `build_executable_labels` marks them `exclude_from_label` rather than dropping them ([labels.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/preprocessing/labels.py:68), [labels.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/preprocessing/labels.py:107)), and `assemble_ml_ready_dataset` builds splits from all base `signal_date` values ([ml_dataset.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/preprocessing/ml_dataset.py:445)). The ML gate checks artifacts, keys, hashes, and policy, but not label completeness by cutoff ([production.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/preprocessing/production.py:113)). Add a hard training cutoff before writing `features.parquet`, `labels.parquet`, and `splits.parquet`, plus a gate assertion that max training `signal_date <= ml_training_label_end`. Put prediction-only rows in a separate artifact or directory.

4. **Medium: Public interface breaks documented flags without a migration path.**
The plan introduces `--mode daily-refresh|monthly-refresh|full-refresh` ([data-plan.md](/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md:12)) but does not preserve or explicitly deprecate the current documented `--refresh-monthly` and `--refresh-raw-store` flows. Those are in current project guidance ([AGENTS.md](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/AGENTS.md:47), [AGENTS.md](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/AGENTS.md:53)) and implemented in the current production CLI ([cli_build_production_dataset.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_build_production_dataset.py:51)). Keep them as aliases for at least one transition or require the same patch to update all docs/tests/callers and add compatibility tests.

5. **Medium: SSE-only date resolution is too narrow for an all-A-share pipeline.**
The plan specifies the SSE official calendar as the resolver source ([data-plan.md](/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md:63)). The project validates SSE, SZSE, and BSE listed counts in Tushare validation ([cli_validate_tushare.py](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/src/quantresearch/cli/cli_validate_tushare.py:21)), while execution and benchmark artifacts consume stock, ETF, and benchmark data with different endpoint lags. SSE is a good base calendar, but the resolver should also verify Tushare `trade_cal` and endpoint freshness for every consumed family before treating a date as complete. The specific 2026-05-02 fixture is correct: SSE says Labor Day closure is May 1-5, 2026 and normal trading resumes May 6 (SSE source: https://www.sse.com.cn/disclosure/announcement/general/c/c_20260423_10816345.shtml).

6. **Low: The integration test wording can produce false positives.**
The plan says no deprecated console script names should appear in the production execution path ([data-plan.md](/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md:156)), but those names must remain in `pyproject.toml`, docs, bypass errors, and tests ([pyproject.toml](/Users/roy/Documents/quant/pyproject.toml:24)). Make this an AST or module-boundary assertion against `cli_build_production_dataset.py` and the new orchestrator internals: no `subprocess`, no `_uv_command`, no `uv run`, and no direct public CLI `main()` calls.

**Overall**
The plan is directionally strong and addresses the current biggest defects: hardcoded `20260424`, subprocess orchestration, stale defaults, and unsafe direct writers. The main changes needed are tighter mode semantics, a clean execution-vs-ML contract, explicit training/prediction artifact separation, and a compatibility path for existing flags.","findings":[{"id":"F1","severity":"high","confidence":0.88,"title":"Daily default scope may execute heavy monthly/full non-L2 work","location":"/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md:15","evidence":"Plan says all non-Level-2 families are orchestrated and appear by default; current non-L2 reference code loops all stock codes and per-code endpoint batches with sleeps in src/quantresearch/data/non_l2_reference.py:227 and :318, while official source discovery performs live downloads in src/quantresearch/preprocessing/official_sources.py:151.","recommendation":"Add an explicit per-mode family matrix with execute/probe/reuse/skip/block behavior. Keep daily-refresh limited to daily/execution-required sources; make slow monthly/full families reused or optional unless their consuming artifact is requested.","actionable":true},{"id":"F2","severity":"high","confidence":0.82,"title":"Execution-ready manifest still implies gated ML when ML may be skipped","location":"/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md:153","evidence":"Plan allows execution-ready success with ML skipped, but cli_build_execution_ready_dataset.py records ml_ready_dir as an input and execution_ready.py declares source_boundary='curated_daily_panels_plus_gated_monthly_ml_ready'.","recommendation":"Either remove ML-ready as an execution-ready input, or distinguish current skipped ML output from a previous gated ML training context in the manifest and gate contract.","actionable":true},{"id":"F3","severity":"high","confidence":0.86,"title":"Incomplete labels can still be materialized inside gated ML artifacts","location":"/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md:59","evidence":"labels.py marks missing exit rows as exclude_from_label rather than dropping them, ml_dataset.py builds splits from all signal dates, and production.py gate validation does not enforce max signal_date <= ml_training_label_end or complete label exits.","recommendation":"Filter training features/labels/splits to label_complete_through before writing gated ML artifacts. Write prediction-only rows separately and add gate tests for cutoff and missing-exit exclusion.","actionable":true},{"id":"F4","severity":"medium","confidence":0.79,"title":"New public interface omits compatibility for existing production flags","location":"/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md:108","evidence":"Current AGENTS.md documents --refresh-monthly and --refresh-raw-store, and cli_build_production_dataset.py implements those flags.
